items.prompt: Why is multivariate or copula-based modeling used in synthetic data generation?
choices: To ignore all relationships between variables
To preserve correlations and dependencies among variables
To guarantee zero privacy risk without validation
items.explain: Sections 10.3.2–10.3.4 emphasize capturing inter-variable relationships for realistic synthetic data.

items.prompt: Bootstrapping draws samples with replacement from an original dataset.
items.explain: Section 10.3.3 defines bootstrapping as resampling with replacement, allowing repeated and omitted points.
Example 10.9 bootstraps a small purchase-history dataset to create larger synthetic training data.

items.prompt: How does regression-based synthesis generate a dependent variable?
items.rubric: Mention sampling predictors and using a fitted regression model (with error term) to predict the outcome.
items.sample_answer: Sample values for independent predictors, apply the trained regression equation (including error), and produce synthetic values of the dependent variable.

items.prompt: Example 10.10 uses macro predictors such as interest rates and inflation to project synthetic GDP growth.
items.explain: Example 10.10 describes regression-based GDP synthesis from interest rates, inflation, and government spending.
